# 📄 Scout Digest — May 10, 2026

**15 papers surfaced out of 561 reviewed**  
**Threshold:** ≥7


## Also Noteworthy

- **[Automated alignment is harder than you think](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06390)** — 9/10. Directly argues that automated AI alignment research using agents is harder than assumed, identifying systematic failure modes in scalable oversight and fuzzy task supervision—highly relevant to AI safety and alignment research. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.06390v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.NKAPLi0kBR7z4-OcSHUazMTYbW9nAV0z-to3HhgHpM0) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.NKAPLi0kBR7z4-OcSHUazMTYbW9nAV0z-to3HhgHpM0)- **[Teaching Claude why](https://www.anthropic.com/research/teaching-claude-why)** — 8/10 · Source: Anthropic · Watchlist: Anthropic. Directly addresses agentic misalignment reduction from Anthropic, a priority organization, touching core AI safety and alignment interests. ([PDF](https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/bf10f64990cfda0ba858290be7b8cc6317685f47.pdf))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.I1f0nQVrS-Z1_BqceUGqiapeEB8hSuUqNdzFufwmdwY) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.I1f0nQVrS-Z1_BqceUGqiapeEB8hSuUqNdzFufwmdwY)- **[Recursive Agent Optimization](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06639)** — 8/10. Directly advances agent architecture research by introducing a novel RL-based training method for recursive multi-agent systems with inference-time scaling, highly relevant to agent planning and multi-agent collaboration. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.06639v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.9kqlmoqiRfA1ByvBaFONKsC6ecXOwB6Jvajq_tn8vSQ) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.9kqlmoqiRfA1ByvBaFONKsC6ecXOwB6Jvajq_tn8vSQ)- **[Crafting Reversible SFT Behaviors in Large Language Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06632)** — 8/10. Proposes mechanistic methods to localize and selectively suppress SFT-induced behaviors in LLMs, directly relevant to interpretability, controllability, and AI safety. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.06632v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.slb82md7xd_b1v2cZwtr0-7bALiKgromqkSy9CcvLjU) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.slb82md7xd_b1v2cZwtr0-7bALiKgromqkSy9CcvLjU)- **[Natural Language Autoencoders](https://www.anthropic.com/research/natural-language-autoencoders)** — 8/10 · Source: Anthropic · Watchlist: Anthropic. Anthropic work on encoding Claude's internal representations into natural language is directly relevant to interpretability and mechanistic understanding of LLMs, though the abstract is too sparse to assess full novelty. ([PDF](https://www-cdn.anthropic.com/963373e433e489a87a10c823c52a0a013e9172dd.pdf))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.mTELuno4NE9paToBcuQhF9YJKIwnBZm-1sAucPiXdcA) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.mTELuno4NE9paToBcuQhF9YJKIwnBZm-1sAucPiXdcA)- **[Instrumental Choices: Measuring the Propensity of LLM Agents to Pursue Instrumental Behaviors](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06490)** — 8/10. Directly measures instrumental convergence behaviors in LLM agents—a core AI safety concern—with a realistic benchmark across ten frontier models, providing empirical evidence on dangerous agentic behaviors. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.06490v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.Mk-YHEk_HOYqL3Sve1KnLMSY-FnJ9yEjo5HxtZg_tNs) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.Mk-YHEk_HOYqL3Sve1KnLMSY-FnJ9yEjo5HxtZg_tNs)- **[Running Codex safely at OpenAI](https://openai.com/index/running-codex-safely)** — 8/10 · Source: OpenAI · Watchlist: OpenAI. Directly addresses safe deployment of a coding agent (Codex) by OpenAI, covering sandboxing, network policies, and agent-native telemetry — highly relevant to AI safety and agent architectures from a priority organization. ([PDF](https://openai.com/index/running-codex-safely))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.Go57gp4jYnO37e89TEaSdn7kmVAfyUlooxCiPcSPJgA) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.Go57gp4jYnO37e89TEaSdn7kmVAfyUlooxCiPcSPJgA)- **[Beyond Accuracy: Policy Invariance as a Reliability Test for LLM Safety Judges](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06161)** — 8/10. Directly addresses reliability and failure modes of LLM-as-a-Judge safety evaluators for agents, introducing policy invariance as a key reliability criterion with practical audit tools. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.06161v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.Sv1ZJFZdOIL5hM2EkQEdUomFLms3_8kYj9dVMBEiHak) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.Sv1ZJFZdOIL5hM2EkQEdUomFLms3_8kYj9dVMBEiHak)- **[Stateful Agent Backdoor](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.06158)** — 8/10. Introduces a novel stateful, multi-session backdoor attack on LLM-based agents, directly relevant to agent security, adversarial robustness, and AI safety failure modes. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.06158v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.81v2fJVuVw5cmS0aYs9EZZffTmpoqdnZT1JK2aRNa70) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.81v2fJVuVw5cmS0aYs9EZZffTmpoqdnZT1JK2aRNa70)- **[Safety Anchor: Defending Harmful Fine-tuning via Geometric Bottlenecks](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05995)** — 8/10. Directly addresses defending LLM safety alignment against harmful fine-tuning attacks via a geometric bottleneck regularization approach, which is highly relevant to alignment robustness and safe training methods. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.05995v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.ZgS1NQYeUttyZq-H8l_gqis6hgZGNc8hUQTtQ4lZD6c) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.ZgS1NQYeUttyZq-H8l_gqis6hgZGNc8hUQTtQ4lZD6c)- **[TACT: Mitigating Overthinking and Overacting in Coding Agents via Activation Steering](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05980)** — 8/10. Directly addresses agent failure modes (overthinking, overacting) in coding agents using activation steering, combining interpretability with agent reliability in a novel and practically useful way. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.05980v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.Hd0UN5bihcRVsygt96BwMFky0-EBLnws6Fxka321VVU) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.Hd0UN5bihcRVsygt96BwMFky0-EBLnws6Fxka321VVU)- **[LoopTrap: Termination Poisoning Attacks on LLM Agents](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05846)** — 8/10. LoopTrap directly studies adversarial attacks on LLM agent termination logic, introducing a red-teaming framework for agent failure modes—highly relevant to agent safety and robustness. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.05846v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.0QpzPcQx3VnwDUED8P22hygciTqcyALcLfyqdGaqvIc) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.0QpzPcQx3VnwDUED8P22hygciTqcyALcLfyqdGaqvIc)- **[Evaluation Awareness in Language Models Has Limited Effect on Behaviour](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05835)** — 8/10. Empirically tests whether evaluation awareness in reasoning models causes strategic behavior or alignment tampering, directly addressing a key AI safety concern about deceptive alignment. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.05835v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.ggQeesixucTrdlvfcRgwQtnRMAyXeCjXWNmljq2Sfew) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.ggQeesixucTrdlvfcRgwQtnRMAyXeCjXWNmljq2Sfew)- **[More Is Not Always Better: Cross-Component Interference in LLM Agent Scaffolding](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05716)** — 8/10. Directly studies agent scaffolding component interactions and failure modes, providing empirical evidence that more components can degrade performance—highly relevant to agent architecture design and evaluation. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.05716v1))
 [👍 Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/up?.5wDBt3fC9njyeBapMBrV2_nxYClw3vLAoFF6FYvrFXI) · [👎 Not Interesting](http://100.78.145.29:8787/feedback/down?.5wDBt3fC9njyeBapMBrV2_nxYClw3vLAoFF6FYvrFXI)- **[SafeHarbor: Hierarchical Memory-Augmented Guardrail for LLM Agent Safety](https://arxiv.org/abs/2605.05704)** — 8/10. Directly addresses LLM agent safety guardrails with a novel memory-augmented framework that balances safety refusal with utility, tackling the over-refusal problem in agent tool use. ([PDF](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2605.05704v1))
